name: Widemouth Bay

dog_rules.summer: Restricted 09:00-18:00 between Easter and 30 September on the main beach. Black Rock end remains year-round dog-friendly.

parking: Large pay-and-display car park at the north and south ends; free off-season
family_suitability: Excellent — wide flat beach, lifeguarded section in summer, surf school on site
opening_hours: Beach access at all times; lifeguard cover Easter-October
hero_image_alt: Wide flat surf beach with cliffs at the north end
editorial: The closest beach to the estate and the one most guests visit first. Big skies, surf school for beginners, lifeguarded in summer.

name: Black Rock Beach
family_suitability: Good — pebbles and rock pools at low tide; not a swimming beach
hero_image_alt: Rocky cove at the south end of Widemouth Bay
editorial: The estate's go-to year-round dog beach. Rock pools at low tide and a cliff-top walk back.

name: Sandymouth Beach
parking: National Trust car park, pay-and-display (free for NT members)
family_suitability: Excellent — sandy at low tide, rock pools, NT cafe
opening_hours: Beach access at all times; cafe Easter-October
hero_image_alt: Sandymouth beach at low tide with cliffs
editorial: National Trust beach — wide sand at low tide, cliffs above, a small cafe in the summer. Year-round dog access.

name: Bude Canal Towpath

dog_rules.summer: Dogs welcome on lead near the sea-lock; off-lead permitted on quieter towpath sections

parking: Crescent and Falcon Yard pay-and-display car parks
family_suitability: Excellent — flat, pram and wheelchair friendly, cafe and SUP hire on site
hero_image_alt: Flat canal towpath leading inland from the Bude sea-lock
editorial: The historic Bude Canal — the only sea-lock canal in England. Two miles of flat towpath, SUP hire from the canal head, a good ice cream van.

name: Summerleaze Beach
parking: Pay-and-display in front of the beach
family_suitability: Excellent — wide sand, Bude sea pool at the north end, lifeguarded
hero_image_alt: Wide sandy beach with the Bude sea pool at the cliff base
editorial: The town beach — wide sand, the famous tidal sea pool at the north end, an ice cream queue worth joining. Dogs allowed outside the summer ban window.

name: Tintagel Castle

dog_rules.summer: Dogs on a lead welcome across the site

parking: Pay-and-display in the village; long walk down to the site
family_suitability: Moderate — long descent and the new bridge requires comfortable shoes; small children may struggle with the steps
hero_image_alt: Tintagel castle ruins and footbridge at the headland
editorial: English Heritage Arthurian site — the new bridge across the chasm is the photograph. Allow a full half-day.

name: Boscastle Harbour

dog_rules.summer: Dogs welcome along the harbour wall and SWCP

family_suitability: Excellent — flat harbour walk, dog-friendly pubs, Museum of Witchcraft and Magic
hero_image_alt: Boscastle harbour mouth with stone breakwaters
editorial: Old smugglers' harbour — narrow entrance, stone-faced cottages, the Cobweb Inn for the post-walk pint. The Museum of Witchcraft and Magic is the wet-day option.

name: Museum of Witchcraft and Magic
family_suitability: Good — best for ages 10+; some exhibits not for young children
hero_image_alt: Stone-faced museum building on Boscastle quayside
editorial: World's largest collection of folk magic and witchcraft objects — the wet-day reason to visit Boscastle.

name: Bude Castle Heritage Centre

dog_rules.summer: Dogs on a lead welcome in grounds; not inside the heritage centre

family_suitability: Excellent — free admission, small museum, garden cafe, family-friendly exhibits
hero_image_alt: Bude Castle building beside the canal
editorial: Free local-history museum next to the canal head — half an hour of stories about Sir Goldsworthy Gurney, Bude's nineteenth-century inventor.

name: Crackington Haven
parking: Small pay-and-display next to the beach
family_suitability: Good — short walk to the sand, pub above, rock pools
hero_image_alt: Cove framed by tall striped cliffs at Crackington
editorial: A small cove framed by the highest cliffs on the south-west coast. The Coombe Barton Inn above is dog-friendly all year.

name: Duckpool Beach
parking: Small free car park at the head of the lane
family_suitability: Moderate — pebbles, rock pools at low tide, no facilities
hero_image_alt: Pebble cove and stream at Duckpool
editorial: Quiet pebble cove with a freshwater stream running across the beach. The pebble surface keeps the summer crowds in Widemouth.

name: Tamar Lakes
parking: Free at the Upper Tamar Lake car park
family_suitability: Excellent — flat 2-mile loop, cafe, kayak hire, sailing club
opening_hours: Always accessible; cafe and kayak hire 09:30-17:00 in season
editorial: Twin reservoirs straddling the Devon-Cornwall border. The Upper Lake is open for SUP, sailing and family kayak hire. The Lower Lake is a nature reserve.
